sections.title: Syntax Overview
sections.content: PD2 item filters control how items are displayed on the ground and in your inventory. Filters use a simple rule-based syntax where each line defines conditions to match and an output to display.

subsections.title: Basic Rule Structure
subsections.content:
Every filter rule follows this format:

ItemDisplay[conditions]: output

The conditions inside the brackets determine which items the rule matches. The output after the colon determines how the item is displayed. If the output is empty, the item is hidden.

// Show Shako with custom name
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT HELM]: %GOLD%Shako %WHITE%- %NAME%

// Hide low-tier normal items
ItemDisplay[NMAG NORM !ETH]:

// Show all Runes with special notification
ItemDisplay[RUNE>0]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% Rune %MAP-0A%

A rule with no output (nothing after the colon) hides the matched items. A rule with %NAME% in the output keeps the item's original name.

subsections.title: Rule Processing
subsections.content:
Rules are processed top-to-bottom and first match wins. Once an item matches a rule, no further rules are checked for that item (unless %CONTINUE% is used).

// This rule matches first — Ber shows as "Ber Rune"
ItemDisplay[RUNE=30]: %ORANGE%Ber Rune

// This would never match Ber because the above already caught it
ItemDisplay[RUNE>20]: %ORANGE%High Rune
Tip: Place more specific rules above general ones. If a general rule comes first, it will catch items before your specific rules can match them.

subsections.title: Comments
subsections.content:
Comments start with // and are ignored by the game. They can appear on their own line or after a rule's output.

// This is a full-line comment

ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: %ORANGE%Zod Rune  // This is an inline comment

// You can also use comments to temporarily disable rules:
// ItemDisplay[NMAG NORM]:

subsections.title: File Format
subsections.content:
Filter files use the .filter extension. Files should be saved in UTF-8 encoding, though ANSI may be needed for certain special characters to render correctly in-game.

Filters are stored in the ProjectD2/filters/ directory. The game looks for filters in this location at launch.

subsections.title: File Loading Order
subsections.content:
PD2 loads filter files in this priority order:

  1. Launcher selection — the filter chosen in the PD2 launcher takes priority
  2. loot.filter — if present, this file is loaded as the active filter
  3. default.filter — fallback if no other filter is specified
You can switch filters in-game using the /reload command, which re-reads the active filter file without restarting.

sections.title: Aliases
sections.content: Aliases allow you to define reusable text shortcuts that are expanded at filter load time. They work as a simple find-and-replace mechanism.

subsections.title: Alias Syntax
subsections.content:
Aliases are defined with the Alias keyword:

Alias[TextToReplace]: TextToBeInserted

When the filter loads, every occurrence of TextToReplace in subsequent rules is replaced with TextToBeInserted. Aliases can be defined anywhere in the file — they are processed before rules are evaluated.

// Define color aliases
Alias[HiRune]: %ORANGE%%MAP-0A%%NOTIFY-1%%BORDER-0A%
Alias[MidRune]: %YELLOW%%DOT-09%
Alias[LoRune]: %WHITE%

// Use them in rules
ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: HiRune Zod Rune
ItemDisplay[RUNE=30]: HiRune Ber Rune
ItemDisplay[RUNE=25]: MidRune Gul Rune
ItemDisplay[RUNE=1]: LoRune El Rune

subsections.title: Practical Alias Examples
subsections.content:
// Toggle alias — change TRUE to FALSE to disable a group of rules
Alias[ShowNormals]: TRUE
ItemDisplay[ShowNormals NMAG NORM ELT]: %NAME%

// Shorten common condition sets
Alias[IsGoodUnique]: UNI ID !ETH
Alias[IsEthElite]: ETH ELT NMAG

ItemDisplay[IsGoodUnique WEAPON]: %GOLD%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[IsEthElite SUP WEAPON]: %GREEN%Eth %NAME%

// Reusable output fragments
Alias[ShowDef]: {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %WHITE%%DEF%}
Alias[ShowSock]: {%NL%%BLUE%Max: %WHITE%%MAXSOCKETS%}

ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT ARMOR]: %NAME% ShowDef ShowSock
Warning: Alias names are case-sensitive and must not conflict with existing filter keywords. Avoid names like RUNE, MAG, or other reserved words.

sections.title: Filter Levels
sections.content: Filter levels let you create multiple visibility tiers within a single filter. Users can cycle through levels in-game to show or hide different categories of items.

subsections.title: Defining Filter Levels
subsections.content:
Filter levels are defined with the ItemDisplayFilterName[] syntax. You can define up to 12 levels (plus the default Level 0).

ItemDisplayFilterName[1]: Relaxed
ItemDisplayFilterName[2]: Default
ItemDisplayFilterName[3]: Strict
ItemDisplayFilterName[4]: Uber Strict

Level 0 is always Show All Items and cannot be renamed. It shows every item regardless of filter rules.

Players cycle through filter levels in-game using the assigned hotkey (default: Numpad 0).

subsections.title: FILTLVL Condition
subsections.content:
Use the FILTLVL value condition to make rules apply only at certain filter levels:

// Only show these items at filter level 1 or lower
ItemDisplay[FILTLVL<2 NMAG ELT]: %NAME%

// Only apply at level 3+
ItemDisplay[FILTLVL>2 MAG !CHARM]: 

// Only at exact level 2
ItemDisplay[FILTLVL=2 MAG JEWELRY]: %NAME%

Rules without a FILTLVL condition apply at all filter levels (except Level 0, which overrides everything).

subsections.title: %TIER-X% for Notification Control
subsections.content:
The %TIER-X% keyword controls the minimum filter level at which an item's notifications (map icons, sounds, text popups) are active.

// Notifications active at level 9+ (default if omitted)
ItemDisplay[RUNE>28]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% %MAP-0A%%TIER-9%

// Notifications only at level 1+ (always on except Show All)
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT]: %GOLD%%NAME% %MAP-09%%TIER-1%

// Notifications at level 5+ only
ItemDisplay[RUNE~20-28]: %YELLOW%%RUNENAME% %DOT-09%%TIER-5%

If %TIER-X% is omitted, notifications default to %TIER-9%. The value X ranges from 0 to 12.

sections.title: Boolean Conditions
sections.content: Boolean conditions are true/false checks placed inside the ItemDisplay[...] brackets. They match items based on quality, type, equipment slot, and other properties. Multiple boolean conditions in a rule are combined with implicit AND logic.

subsections.title: Quality
subsections.content:
CodeDescription
NMAGNon-magical (white/gray name) items
MAGMagic (blue name) items
RARERare (yellow name) items
UNIUnique (gold name) items
SETSet (green name) items
CRAFTCrafted (orange name) items
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT WEAPON]: %GOLD%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[SET HELM]: %GREEN%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[RARE JEWELRY]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

subsections.title: Item Tier
subsections.content:
CodeDescription
NORMNormal tier items
EXCExceptional tier items
ELTElite tier items
ItemDisplay[NMAG NORM !ETH]:      // Hide normal non-magic non-eth
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT]: %NAME%     // Show elite non-magic

subsections.title: Item Properties
subsections.content:
CodeDescription
IDItem is identified
INFItem is ethereal (inferior) — Note: same as ETH for most purposes; prefer ETH
SUPItem is superior
ETHItem is ethereal
RWItem is a runeword
GEMMEDItem has gems/runes/jewels inserted
ItemDisplay[ETH ELT NMAG WEAPON]: %WHITE%Eth %NAME%
ItemDisplay[RW]: %GOLD%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[SUP NMAG !ETH ELT ARMOR]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Superior}

subsections.title: Equipment Groups
subsections.content:
CodeAliasDescription
EQ1HELMHelms, casques, etc.
EQ2CHESTBody armor
EQ3SHIELDShields, shrunken heads
EQ4GLOVESGloves
EQ5BOOTSBoots
EQ6BELTBelts
EQ7CIRCCirclets, coronets, tiaras, diadems
—ARMORAny armor piece (EQ1–EQ7 combined)
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT CHEST]: %NAME%
ItemDisplay[RARE CIRC ID]: %YELLOW%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[SET ARMOR]: %GREEN%%NAME%

subsections.title: Weapon Groups
subsections.content:
CodeAliasDescription
WP1AXEAxes
WP2MACEMaces
WP3SWORDSwords
WP4DAGGERDaggers
WP5THROWINGThrowing weapons
WP6JAVJavelins
WP7SPEARSpears
WP8POLEARMPolearms
WP9BOWBows
WP10XBOWCrossbows
WP11STAFFStaves
WP12WANDWands
WP13SCEPTERScepters
—WEAPONAny weapon (WP1–WP13 combined)
—1HOne-handed weapons
—2HTwo-handed weapons
—CLUBClubs (subset of maces)
—TMACEThrowing maces (subset)
—HAMMERHammers (subset of maces)

subsections.title: Class-Specific Items
subsections.content:
CodeAliasDescription
CL1DRUDruid pelts
CL2BARBarbarian helms
CL3DINPaladin shields
CL4NECNecromancer shrunken heads
CL5SINAssassin claws
CL6SORSorceress orbs
CL7ZONAmazon weapons (bows/javelins/spears)
—CLASSAny class-specific item
ItemDisplay[DIN ELT NMAG]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Pally Shield}
ItemDisplay[SIN ELT RARE ID]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

subsections.title: Miscellaneous
subsections.content:
CodeDescription
JEWELRYRings and amulets
CHARMCharms (small, large, grand)
QUIVERArrows and bolts
MISCMiscellaneous items (keys, organs, essences, etc.)

subsections.title: Mutable (Location/State) Conditions
subsections.content:
These conditions check where or how the item is currently placed:

CodeDescription
SHOPItem is in a shop/vendor window
EQUIPPEDItem is currently equipped by the player
MERCItem is equipped on the mercenary
INVENTORYItem is in the player's inventory
CUBEItem is in the Horadric Cube
STASHItem is in the stash
GROUNDItem is on the ground
Note: Most filter rules operate on ground items. Mutable conditions are useful for in-inventory recoloring and shop filtering.

subsections.title: Character Class Conditions
subsections.content:
These check the class of the current character, not the item type:

CodeDescription
AMAZONCurrent character is an Amazon
ASSASSINCurrent character is an Assassin
BARBARIANCurrent character is a Barbarian
DRUIDCurrent character is a Druid
NECROMANCERCurrent character is a Necromancer
PALADINCurrent character is a Paladin
SORCERESSCurrent character is a Sorceress
// Only show Paladin shields when playing Paladin
ItemDisplay[PALADIN DIN ELT NMAG]: %WHITE%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[!PALADIN DIN NMAG]:

// Show Amazon-specific bows only for Amazon
ItemDisplay[AMAZON ZON UNI]: %GOLD%%NAME%

sections.title: Value Conditions
sections.content: Value conditions compare a numeric property against a value. They are used inside the ItemDisplay[...] brackets alongside boolean conditions.

subsections.title: Operators
subsections.content:
OperatorMeaningExample
=Equal toRUNE=30
<Less thanILVL<25
>Greater thanCLVL>80
~Between (inclusive)RUNE~20-28
ItemDisplay[RUNE=30]: Ber Rune         // Exact match
ItemDisplay[RUNE>28]: %ORANGE%High Rune  // Greater than
ItemDisplay[ILVL<10]: %GRAY%Low Level    // Less than
ItemDisplay[RUNE~20-28]: %YELLOW%Mid    // Between 20 and 28 inclusive

subsections.title: Info Codes
subsections.content:
CodeDescriptionRange/Notes
GOLDGold pile amountMatches gold piles
GEMLEVEL / GEMGem quality level1=Chipped, 2=Flawed, 3=Normal, 4=Flawless, 5=Perfect
GEMTYPEGem type1=Amethyst, 2=Diamond, 3=Emerald, 4=Ruby, 5=Sapphire, 6=Topaz, 7=Skull
RUNERune number1 (El) through 33 (Zod)
QTYStack quantityFor stackable items (keys, arrows, etc.)
LVLREQLevel requirementRequired level to equip
PRICEItem sell priceGold value at vendor
ALVLArea levelLevel of the area where item dropped
CRAFTALVLCrafting area levelEffective alvl for crafting recipes
REROLLALVLReroll area levelEffective alvl for reroll recipes
QLVLQuality levelItem base quality level
ILVLItem levelThe item's internal level
CLVLCharacter levelCurrent character level
DIFFDifficulty0=Normal, 1=Nightmare, 2=Hell
MAPIDMap ID1–175, identifies the current map
MAPTIERMap tier0–5
PREFIXMagic prefix IDUse ~ operator (see note below)
SUFFIXMagic suffix IDUse ~ operator (see note below)
AUTOMODAutomod IDAutomatic modifier (e.g., paladin shield resistances)
FILTLVLCurrent filter level0–12
MAXSOCKETSMaximum possible socketsMax sockets the base can roll
PREFIX/SUFFIX Note: The < and > operators are unreliable for PREFIX and SUFFIX codes. Always use the ~ (between) operator instead: PREFIX~152-152 to match a specific prefix.

subsections.title: Base Stat Codes
subsections.content:
These codes check the base item properties (before modifiers):

CodeDescription
BASEDEFBase defense (unmodified)
BASEMAXDAMBase maximum damage
BASEMINDAMBase minimum damage
BASEMAXTHROWBase max throw damage
BASEMINTHROWBase min throw damage
REQDEXRequired dexterity
REQSTRRequired strength
REQLVLRequired level (base)
UPDEXRequired dex if upgraded
UPSTRRequired str if upgraded
UPLVLRequired level if upgraded
MAXRESMaximum resistance on the base
ALLATTRIBAll attributes bonus
BASEBLOCKBase block chance
HEIGHTInventory height in cells
WIDTHInventory width in cells
AREAInventory area (HEIGHT * WIDTH)
// Show elite armors with high base defense
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT CHEST BASEDEF>500]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Base Def: %EDEF%}

// Show shields with 40+ base block
ItemDisplay[NMAG SHIELD BASEBLOCK>39]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Block: %BASEBLOCK%%}

sections.title: Logical Operators
sections.content: Logical operators control how multiple conditions are combined within a rule's brackets.

subsections.title: AND (Implicit)
subsections.content:
Conditions placed next to each other (separated by spaces) are combined with AND logic. All conditions must be true for the rule to match.

// Item must be: Unique AND Elite AND Weapon
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT WEAPON]: %GOLD%%NAME%

// Item must be: Non-magic AND Ethereal AND Elite AND Armor
ItemDisplay[NMAG ETH ELT ARMOR]: %WHITE%Eth %NAME%

subsections.title: OR (Parentheses)
subsections.content:
To match if any one of several conditions is true, use parentheses with the OR keyword:

// Match Helms OR Circlets
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT (HELM OR CIRC)]: %NAME%

// Match if item is Unique OR Set
ItemDisplay[(UNI OR SET) ELT WEAPON]: %GOLD%%NAME%

// Match specific runes
ItemDisplay[(RUNE=30 OR RUNE=32 OR RUNE=33)]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME%
Important: The OR keyword only works inside parentheses. You cannot write ItemDisplay[HELM OR CIRC]: — it must be ItemDisplay[(HELM OR CIRC)]:.

subsections.title: NOT (Exclamation Mark)
subsections.content:
Place ! before a condition to negate it:

// Not ethereal
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT !ETH ARMOR]: %NAME%

// Not identified
ItemDisplay[UNI !ID]: %GOLD%Unidentified %NAME%

// NOT can also negate a group in parentheses
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT !(HELM OR CHEST)]: %NAME%

subsections.title: Combining Operators
subsections.content:
You can combine AND, OR, and NOT for complex conditions:

// Elite non-magic weapons that are either ethereal or superior
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT WEAPON (ETH OR SUP)]: %WHITE%%NAME%

// Unique or Set, Elite, not ethereal
ItemDisplay[(UNI OR SET) ELT !ETH ARMOR]: %GOLD%%NAME%

// Runes 28-33 OR any unique elite weapon
ItemDisplay[(RUNE~28-33 OR (UNI ELT WEAPON))]: %ORANGE%%NAME%

// Class items for specific classes when playing that class
ItemDisplay[PALADIN DIN ELT (NMAG OR RARE)]: %NAME%

sections.title: Named Attributes
sections.content: Named attributes let you check specific item stats in conditions. They are used inside the brackets just like value conditions, comparing against a numeric threshold.

subsections.title: Attribute Reference
subsections.content:
CodeAliasDescription
SOCKSOCKETSNumber of sockets
DEF—Total defense
ED—Enhanced damage/defense (from affixes only, NOT rw/sockets)
EDEF—Enhanced defense (includes all sources)
EDAM—Enhanced damage (includes all sources)
MAXDUR—Maximum durability
AR—Attack rating
RES—All resistances
FRES—Fire resistance
CRES—Cold resistance
LRES—Lightning resistance
PRES—Poison resistance
FRW—Faster run/walk
IAS—Increased attack speed
FCR—Faster cast rate
FHR—Faster hit recovery
FBR—Faster block rate
MINDMG—Minimum damage
MAXDMG—Maximum damage
STR—Strength bonus
DEX—Dexterity bonus
LIFE—Life bonus
MANA—Mana bonus
MFIND—Magic find %
GFIND—Gold find %
MAEK—Mana after each kill
DTM—Damage taken goes to mana %
REPLIFE—Replenish life
REPAIR—Repairs durability (1 in X seconds)
ARPER—Attack rating per level
FOOLS—Fool's mod (max damage per level)

subsections.title: Stat Addition (Combining Attributes)
subsections.content:
You can add multiple attribute codes together using + and compare the sum:

// Total elemental resistances above 79
ItemDisplay[RARE ARMOR ID FRES+CRES+LRES+PRES>79]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%GREEN%Res: %RES%}

// FCR + FHR combo
ItemDisplay[RARE JEWELRY ID FCR+FHR>30]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

// Life + Mana total
ItemDisplay[RARE CHARM ID LIFE+MANA>35]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

This works with any named attributes or STAT/MULTI codes (covered in the next section).

subsections.title: ED vs EDEF/EDAM
subsections.content:
This is a common source of confusion:

  • ED — Enhanced Damage/Defense from affixes only. This does NOT include bonuses from runewords, socketed gems, or jewels.
  • EDEF — Enhanced Defense from all sources, including runewords and socketed items.
  • EDAM — Enhanced Damage from all sources, including runewords and socketed items.
// For checking runeword defense, use EDEF
ItemDisplay[RW ARMOR EDEF>200]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %DEF%}

// For checking rare/magic affix ED, use ED
ItemDisplay[RARE WEAPON ID ED>300]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

// For checking total enhanced damage, use EDAM
ItemDisplay[UNI WEAPON ID EDAM>250]: %GOLD%%NAME%
Shortcut codes that can be used directly in conditions without STAT/MULTI: STR, DEX, LIFE, MANA, FRES, CRES, LRES, PRES, EDEF, EDAM, FCR, AR, REPLIFE.

sections.title: STAT Codes
sections.content: For attributes not covered by named shortcuts, you can use the STAT# and CHARSTAT# codes to check any item or character stat by its numeric ID.

subsections.title: STAT# Format
subsections.content:
Use STAT followed by the stat number to check an item's stat value:

// STAT0 = Strength
ItemDisplay[RARE ARMOR ID STAT0>20]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

// STAT39 = Faster Cast Rate
ItemDisplay[RARE JEWELRY ID STAT39>9]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

// STAT80 = Magic Find
ItemDisplay[UNI ID STAT80>30]: %GOLD%%NAME%

Common STAT codes:

STAT#Description
STAT0Strength
STAT2Dexterity
STAT3Vitality
STAT7Life
STAT9Mana
STAT16Enhanced defense %
STAT17Enhanced damage % (min)
STAT19Attack rating
STAT20Increased blocking
STAT39Faster cast rate
STAT43Faster hit recovery
STAT48Fire resistance
STAT49Max fire resistance
STAT50Cold resistance
STAT52Lightning resistance
STAT54Poison resistance
STAT67Faster run/walk
STAT74Replenish life
STAT76Life after kill
STAT78Magic find
STAT80Gold find
STAT93Increased attack speed
Note: There are 500+ stat codes in D2. Consult the PD2 Wiki — Item Filtering page for the complete list.

subsections.title: CHARSTAT# (Character Stats)
subsections.content:
CHARSTAT# checks a stat on the character, not the item. This is useful for conditional display based on your current character's stats.

// Show strength items if character has low strength
ItemDisplay[RARE ID STAT0>15 CHARSTAT0<150]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%GREEN%+%STAT0% STR}

// Check gold amounts
// CHARSTAT14 = gold carried by character
// CHARSTAT15 = gold in stash
ItemDisplay[GOLD>5000 CHARSTAT14<100000]: %WHITE%%NAME%
CodeDescription
CHARSTAT0Character strength
CHARSTAT2Character dexterity
CHARSTAT3Character vitality
CHARSTAT7Character life
CHARSTAT9Character mana
CHARSTAT14Gold carried
CHARSTAT15Gold in stash

sections.title: Skills
sections.content: Filter conditions can check for skill bonuses on items. There are several code formats for different types of skill bonuses.

subsections.title: All Skills (ALLSK)
subsections.content:
Checks for +X to All Skills:

// Items with +2 or more to all skills
ItemDisplay[UNI ID ALLSK>1]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%%PURPLE%+%STAT127% All Skills}

subsections.title: Class Skills (CLSK)
subsections.content:
CLSK# checks +X to (class) Skills. The class number mapping:

CodeClass
CLSK0Amazon
CLSK1Sorceress
CLSK2Necromancer
CLSK3Paladin
CLSK4Barbarian
CLSK5Druid
CLSK6Assassin
// Amulet with +2 Sorceress skills
ItemDisplay[RARE JEWELRY ID CLSK1>1]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%PURPLE%+%SK83% Sorc}

// Any class skill +2
ItemDisplay[RARE ID (CLSK0>1 OR CLSK1>1 OR CLSK2>1 OR CLSK3>1 OR CLSK4>1 OR CLSK5>1 OR CLSK6>1)]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

subsections.title: Skill Tabs (TABSK)
subsections.content:
TABSK# checks +X to a specific skill tab. Each class has 3 tabs (numbered 0–20):

Tab IDsClassTab 1Tab 2Tab 3
0–2Amazon0: Bow & Crossbow1: Passive & Magic2: Javelin & Spear
3–5Sorceress3: Fire4: Lightning5: Cold
6–8Necromancer6: Curses7: Poison & Bone8: Summoning
9–11Paladin9: Combat10: Offensive Auras11: Defensive Auras
12–14Barbarian12: Warcries13: Combat Masteries14: Combat Skills
15–17Druid15: Summoning16: Shape Shifting17: Elemental
18–20Assassin18: Traps19: Shadow Disciplines20: Martial Arts
// Circlet with +Sorc Fire skills
ItemDisplay[RARE CIRC ID TABSK3>1]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%RED%+Fire Skills}

// Paladin Combat tab bonus
ItemDisplay[RARE DIN ID TABSK9>1]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

subsections.title: Individual Skills (SK#) and Oskills (OS#)
subsections.content:
SK# checks for +X to a specific skill. OS# checks for oskill (non-class skill) bonuses. CHSK# checks for skill charges.

// Item with +Teleport (SK54)
ItemDisplay[UNI ID SK54>0]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%+Teleport}

// Oskill Teleport
ItemDisplay[RARE ID OS54>0]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%+Teleport}

// Charges of Lower Resist (CHSK91)
ItemDisplay[UNI ID CHSK91>0]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%%RED%Lower Res Charges}

subsections.title: MULTI for Skills
subsections.content:
The MULTI code is used for stats that require two parameters (stat ID and sub-parameter). For skills, the relevant MULTI formats are:

FormatDescription
MULTI83,class_id+X to class skills (same as CLSK#)
MULTI97,skill_id+X to oskill (same as OS#)
MULTI107,skill_id+X to single skill (same as SK#)
MULTI188,tab_id+X to skill tab (same as TABSK#)
// These two are equivalent:
ItemDisplay[UNI ID CLSK1>1]: %GOLD%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[UNI ID MULTI83,1>1]: %GOLD%%NAME%

// MULTI is useful when combining with stat addition:
ItemDisplay[RARE ID MULTI107,54+MULTI107,59>2]: %YELLOW%%NAME%

sections.title: Output Keywords
sections.content: Output keywords are placed after the colon in a filter rule. They control the item's display name and provide dynamic information about the item.

subsections.title: Name Keywords
subsections.content:
KeywordDescription
%NAME%The item's full name (or custom name from a previous %CONTINUE% rule)
%BASENAME%The item's base type name (e.g., "Dusk Shroud" instead of "Enigma Dusk Shroud")
%CODE%The item's internal 3-character code (e.g., "utp" for Archon Plate)

subsections.title: Value Keywords
subsections.content:
These output the numeric value of a property in the item's display text:

KeywordDescription
%ILVL%Item level
%ALVL%Area level
%CRAFTALVL%Craft area level
%REROLLALVL%Reroll area level
%LVLREQ%Level requirement
%PRICE%Sell price
%SELLPRICE%Sell price (alias)
%BUYPRICE%Buy price (shop items)
%QTY%Stack quantity
%RANGE%Weapon range
%WPNSPD%Weapon speed modifier
%RUNENUM%Rune number (1–33)
%RUNENAME%Rune name (El, Eld, etc.)
%GEMLEVEL%Gem quality level (1–5)
%GEMTYPE%Gem type number (1–7)
%MAXSOCKETS%Maximum possible sockets
%SOCKETS%Current socket count
%DEF%Total defense
%ED%Enhanced damage/defense (affix only)
%EDEF%Enhanced defense (all sources)
%EDAM%Enhanced damage (all sources)
%AR%Attack rating
%RES%All resistances
%STR%Strength
%DEX%Dexterity
%STAT#%Value of stat # (any stat by number)
%SK#%Value of skill # bonus
%HEIGHT%Inventory height
%WIDTH%Inventory width
%AREA%Inventory area
%BASEBLOCK%Base block chance
%MAXRES%Max resistance
%ALLATTRIB%All attributes bonus
// Show defense and sockets on elite bases
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT ARMOR]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %DEF% %NL%%BLUE%Max Sockets: %MAXSOCKETS%}

// Show rune number for sorting
ItemDisplay[RUNE>0]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% [%RUNENUM%]

// Show item level for crafting
ItemDisplay[RARE JEWELRY]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%GRAY%ilvl: %ILVL%}

sections.title: Colors
sections.content: Color keywords change the text color of everything that follows them in the output. Colors can be used in both the item name and the description.

subsections.title: Color Reference
subsections.content:
KeywordColorNotes
%WHITE%WhiteDefault text color
%GRAY%GrayUsed for socketed/ethereal items
%BLUE%BlueMagic item color
%YELLOW%YellowRare item color
%GOLD%GoldUnique item color
%GREEN%GreenSet item color
%DARK_GREEN%Dark Green
%TAN%Tan
%BLACK%BlackEffectively invisible on dark backgrounds
%PURPLE%PurpleCrafted item color
%RED%Red
%ORANGE%OrangeCommonly used for runes
%CORAL%CoralGlide/HD Text only
%SAGE%SageGlide/HD Text only
%TEAL%TealGlide/HD Text only
%LIGHT_GRAY%Light GrayGlide/HD Text only
Renderer Note: %CORAL%, %SAGE%, %TEAL%, and %LIGHT_GRAY% only work with the Glide wrapper or HD Text mod. In the default Direct3D renderer, they display as white.
Character cost: Each color code consumes 3 internal characters toward the name/description limits. Plan your colors carefully when building long item names.

subsections.title: Color Usage Examples
subsections.content:
// Multi-color item name
ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: %ORANGE%Zod %WHITE%Rune %GRAY%[33]

// Color in descriptions
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT CHEST]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %WHITE%%DEF% %NL%%BLUE%Max: %WHITE%%MAXSOCKETS%}

// Using color to create visual hierarchy
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT WEAPON ID]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%%RED%ED: %WHITE%%EDAM%%% %NL%%BLUE%AR: %WHITE%%AR%}

sections.title: Descriptions
sections.content: Descriptions add extra text lines below the item name when you hover over it. They appear inside curly braces {} in the output section of a rule.

subsections.title: Description Syntax
subsections.content:
Text inside {} braces in the output is placed in the item's description area (the tooltip), not the item name.

// Name is "Shako", description shows defense
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT HELM ID]: %GOLD%Shako {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %WHITE%%DEF%}

// Multiple description lines
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT CHEST]: %WHITE%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %WHITE%%DEF% %NL%%BLUE%Max Sock: %WHITE%%MAXSOCKETS% %NL%%GRAY%ilvl: %ILVL%}

subsections.title: Newlines and Conditional Spacing
subsections.content:
CodeDescription
%NL%Newline — adds a line break. Lines build from bottom-up in descriptions.
%CL%Conditional newline — only adds a newline if there is already content on the current line.
%CS%Conditional space — only adds a space if there is already content on the current line.
Bottom-up construction: Description lines are rendered from bottom to top. The first %NL% in your output becomes the bottom line of the tooltip. Each subsequent %NL% adds a line above the previous one.
// This creates:
//   Line 3 (top)
//   Line 2
//   Line 1 (bottom, closest to stats)
ItemDisplay[UNI ID]: %GOLD%%NAME% {%NL%Line 1 %NL%Line 2 %NL%Line 3}

subsections.title: Character Limits
subsections.content:
Item names and descriptions have strict character limits:

ElementDisplayed LimitInternal Limit
Item Name56 characters125 characters (includes color codes)
Description (total)~500 characters total across all description lines

Color codes each consume 3 internal characters, so a name with many color changes will hit the internal limit faster than the displayed limit.

Important: Descriptions ({} content) only work for: identified magic/rare/unique/set/craft items, runewords, and shop items. Unidentified items and plain non-magic items on the ground will not display description text.

sections.title: Notifications
sections.content: Notifications provide visual and audio alerts for important items. They include minimap icons, sounds, and on-screen text popups.

subsections.title: Minimap Icons
subsections.content:
Minimap icons place a colored marker on the minimap at the item's location. There are four sizes:

CodeSizeDescription
%BORDER-XX%LargeLarge bordered square icon
%MAP-XX%MediumMedium square icon
%DOT-XX%SmallSmall dot icon
%PX-XX%TinySingle pixel

XX is a 2-digit hexadecimal color code (00–FF). If omitted, the icon uses the current text color.

// Large orange icon for high runes
ItemDisplay[RUNE>28]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% Rune %BORDER-0A%

// Medium green icon for set items
ItemDisplay[SET ELT]: %GREEN%%NAME% %MAP-84%

// Small blue dot for magic jewels
ItemDisplay[MAG jew]: %BLUE%%NAME% %DOT-97%

// Icon with default text color
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT]: %GOLD%%NAME% %MAP%

Common hex color values:

HexColor
0AOrange
09Yellow
84Green
97Blue
62Gold
1FRed
D0Purple

subsections.title: Sound Notifications
subsections.content:
Play a sound when an item drops:

%SOUNDID-#%

Sound IDs 4714–4729 are custom PoE-inspired notification sounds added by PD2. You can test sounds in-game using the command:

.playsound 4714
// High rune with sound
ItemDisplay[RUNE>28]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% %BORDER-0A%%SOUNDID-4715%

// Unique elite with sound
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT]: %GOLD%%NAME% %MAP-62%%SOUNDID-4714%

subsections.title: Text Notifications
subsections.content:
%NOTIFY-X% displays a text popup on the side of the screen when the item drops. The X value determines the text color.

// Orange text notification
ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: %ORANGE%Zod Rune %NOTIFY-1%

// Disable text notification for a rule
ItemDisplay[MAG CHARM]: %BLUE%%NAME% %NOTIFY-DEAD%

%NOTIFY-DEAD% explicitly disables the text notification for that rule.

subsections.title: Notification Behavior
subsections.content:
Notifications are processed in a second pass, meaning they apply regardless of where the notification keywords appear in the rule output. The item name is determined by the first pass, then notifications are extracted and activated separately.

Tip: Place notification keywords at the end of the output string for readability, though their position does not affect behavior.

subsections.title: %TIER-X% Notification Threshold
subsections.content:
%TIER-X% sets the minimum filter level at which an item's notifications are active. The value X ranges from 0 to 12.

  • If the current filter level is less than X, notifications for that item are suppressed.
  • If omitted, %TIER-9% is used as the default.
// Always notify (filter level 0+, i.e., always)
ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: %ORANGE%Zod %BORDER-0A%%SOUNDID-4715%%TIER-0%

// Only notify at level 5+
ItemDisplay[RUNE~20-25]: %YELLOW%%RUNENAME% %DOT-09%%TIER-5%

// Default behavior (TIER-9, only notifies at level 9+)
ItemDisplay[MAG JEWELRY]: %BLUE%%NAME% %DOT-97%

sections.title: Special Keywords
sections.content: These keywords provide advanced control over filter behavior and item display.

subsections.title: %CONTINUE%
subsections.content:
%CONTINUE% overrides the normal "first match wins" behavior. When a rule's output contains %CONTINUE%, the current output is stored as the item's %NAME%, and rule processing continues to subsequent rules.

Important: %CONTINUE% must appear outside of description braces {}. It will not work inside descriptions.
// Step 1: Add base info to all elite armors, then continue
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT ARMOR]: %WHITE%%NAME% %CONTINUE%

// Step 2: Add socket info (this sees the modified %NAME% from Step 1)
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT ARMOR SOCK>0]: %NAME% %GRAY%(%SOCKETS%) %CONTINUE%

// Step 3: Add defense info and stop
ItemDisplay[NMAG ELT ARMOR BASEDEF>400]: %NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%Def: %DEF%}

This creates a chain: the first rule adds the base name, the second appends socket count, and the third adds a defense description. Each %CONTINUE% passes the accumulated name to the next matching rule.

subsections.title: Practical %CONTINUE% Chaining
subsections.content:
// Build up rune display progressively
ItemDisplay[RUNE>0]: %ORANGE%%RUNENAME% %CONTINUE%
ItemDisplay[RUNE>0]: %NAME% %GRAY%[#%RUNENUM%] %CONTINUE%
ItemDisplay[RUNE>20]: %NAME% %MAP-0A%%SOUNDID-4714%%TIER-3%
ItemDisplay[RUNE~1-20]: %NAME% %DOT-09%

// Add tier labels to unique items
ItemDisplay[UNI NORM]: %GOLD%%NAME% %GRAY%(N) %CONTINUE%
ItemDisplay[UNI EXC]: %GOLD%%NAME% %GRAY%(X) %CONTINUE%
ItemDisplay[UNI ELT]: %GOLD%%NAME% %GRAY%(E) %CONTINUE%
ItemDisplay[UNI ID]: %NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%ilvl: %ILVL%}

subsections.title: Highlight with Spaces
subsections.content:
You can pad item names with spaces for visual emphasis. However, whitespace at the edges of the name is trimmed. To preserve leading/trailing whitespace, use a tab character as an anchor.

// Padding with spaces (tabs at edges preserve spacing)
ItemDisplay[RUNE=33]: %ORANGE%	   Zod Rune   	

// Creating a highlighted/padded appearance
ItemDisplay[RUNE>28]: %ORANGE%	  %RUNENAME% Rune  	 %BORDER-0A%

subsections.title: Shop Item Price Recoloring
subsections.content:
Filter rules can change how items appear in shop windows using the SHOP condition. This is useful for highlighting good deals or items worth buying.

// Highlight staffmods in shop
ItemDisplay[SHOP STAFF MAG SK54>0]: %PURPLE%%NAME%

// Color cheap items differently
ItemDisplay[SHOP PRICE<1000]: %GRAY%%NAME%

// Highlight shop items with useful mods
ItemDisplay[SHOP RARE JEWELRY ID FCR>9]: %YELLOW%%NAME% {%NL%%BLUE%FCR: %STAT39%}

subsections.title: TRUE/FALSE Constants
subsections.content:
TRUE and FALSE are constant conditions that always pass or always fail. They are most useful when combined with aliases to create toggle switches.

// Create toggle aliases
Alias[ShowCharms]: TRUE
Alias[ShowJewels]: TRUE
Alias[ShowScrolls]: FALSE

// Rules that can be toggled by changing the alias
ItemDisplay[ShowCharms MAG CHARM]: %BLUE%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[ShowJewels MAG jew]: %BLUE%%NAME%
ItemDisplay[ShowScrolls (tsc OR isc)]: %NAME%

// To disable charm display, just change the alias:
// Alias[ShowCharms]: FALSE
Tip: This pattern is very popular for creating user-configurable filters. Place all toggle aliases at the top of the file with comments explaining what each controls.
